fundamentals.name: Datadog, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Datadog, Inc. operates an observability and security platform for cloud applications in the United States and internationally. The company's products comprise infrastructure and application performance monitoring, log management, observability pipelines, synthetics, real user monitoring, product analytics, continuous profiler, database monitoring, data observability, LLM observability, error tracking, network monitoring, incident response, workflow automation and App builder, event management, bits AI SRE, cloud cost management, cloud security, code security, cloud SIEM, threat management, sensitive data scanner, and CI visibility. Datadog, Inc. was incorporated in 2010 and is headquartered in New York, New York.

judgment.summary: Datadog shows impressive revenue and earnings growth with strong margins, but trades at a very high valuation and has low operating and profit margins.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat estimates by 18.3%; next earnings date is 2026-08-06.
positives: Revenue growth 32.2% YoY and quarterly earnings growth 113.4% indicate strong top-line and bottom-line expansion.
Gross margin 79.9% is exceptionally high, suggesting efficient service delivery.
Free cash flow (TTM) of $936.73M demonstrates solid cash generation capabilities.
Consensus rating is strong_buy from 47 analysts, with a mean target price near the current spot price.
negatives: Trailing P/E of 560.45 and Price/Sales (TTM) of 21.73 indicate a very high valuation.
Operating margin at 0.8% and Profit margin at 3.7% are surprisingly low given the high gross margin, suggesting high operating expenses.
Return on assets is negative (-0.2%), and return on equity (3.9%) is modest relative to the valuation.
Beta of 1.29 suggests higher volatility than the broader market.

news.paragraph: Datadog recently reported its first US$1 billion quarter and raised its 2026 outlook, signaling strong performance and positive future expectations. This news comes as the company is highlighted as a trending stock, with some analysts, like Dan Ives, adding it to their AI ETF. The strong earnings surprise and raised guidance suggest continued momentum for DDOG.
